prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
English to French: "Le silence qui nous séparait disait plus que n'importe quelle argutie ne le pourrait." - Tricky to render faithfully: "le silence" cannot be literally translated as "argutie". In French, "argutie" refers to a clever response or argument while "le silence" refers to the lack of sound or communication.

English to Japanese: "俺たちの間の言い隠れは、どのような論争よりも多くを表していました."

English to Arabic: "التوصل بينا كَتبَ أَكثر مِنْ أَي ّقضية تُحْسِبْ بِها."
